summary: Stancil argues that the iPhone is remarkable for delivering a fundamentally egalitarian product experience across social strata, unlike Twitter, where each user inhabits a materially different version shaped by identity and prominence. Because these experiences are unmeasurable and often inaccessible, leaders and data practitioners must approach product decisions with humility and seek lived experience beyond dashboards.
money_quotes: Of all of the iPhone’s accomplishments, one of the most remarkable is that it put the same exact piece of technology into the pockets of people in every strata of society.
On any social media platform—and on Twitter in particular—none of us use the same product.
In contrast to the iPhone, Twitter is remarkable in its experiential inequality.
We don’t have to use numbers to be rational.
Our data won’t save us from having to endure those of others.
key_insight: Some products demand humility because their most important user experiences are unequal, unquantifiable, and only knowable through empathy rather than data alone.
